response.justification: This candidate is an accomplished generalist software engineer, but the profile is a poor match for a mid-senior C++ capital-markets role. Hands-on C++ is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) around 2014–2015, with no recent or sustained C++ work and no evidence of C++ on Windows for production financial systems. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience (only MySQL/MongoDB/generic SQL), and no Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain exposure. The recent stack is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which does not satisfy the mandatory requirements.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience with strong testing/TDD and CI/CD discipline
Some prior C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI) and low-latency systems work in DLang
Excellent communication evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months) and roughly a decade old; far short of the 5–10 years required
No SQL Server/Oracle and no Fixed Income or capital-markets/investment-banking domain knowledge
No demonstrated C++ development on Windows for production financial systems
